Rowboat - Zangrilli/Gauriglia - Request.body: Good Afternoon, Please see below email from Lucas's attorneys. Please confirm whether you have access to Quickbooks. Additionally, we are drafting a list of documents to request from Lucas (bank records, ledgers, receivables, etc.). Please let us know if you would like us to request any specific documents. During our call on Tuesday, Lucas's attorneys stated that they are working on putting together some kind of proposal; however, they could not offer a timeline. In the interim, they proposed equal distributions for you and Lucas if you need money prior to the resolution of this matter. If you would like to propose a distribution amount, please let us know and we will convey that offer to his attorneys. We did not get into the details of the allegations Lucas has been making nor did we provide any additional information regarding your allegations against Lucas. The purpose of their call was clearly to establish some sense or normalcy so that operations can continue during this process. To that, please provide a list of items you require from Lucas in order to keep running the business in the manner you did prior to the breakdown of the professional relationship. Please let us know if you have any questions or would like to discuss this in more detail. Sincerely, *Michael T. Michael Sanderson ---------- Forwarded message --------- From: Horvath, Seth Date: Thu, Jun 22, 2023 at 2:09 PM Subject: RE: Rowboat - Zangrilli/Gauriglia - Request for Phone Call To: Jessica Lesko , Thomas Nitschke , Michael Sanderson CC: Tilghman, Richard Dear Counsel: We are in the process of discussing with our client the terms of a buyout offer for Mr. Zangrilli’s ownership interest in Rowboat Creative LLC. Meanwhile, our client is concerned about protecting the company’s financial well-being and would like to reach an agreement regarding draws, and credit-card usage, while the discussions regarding the buyout are pending. Mr. Guariglia will reciprocate. These arrangements are in the best interests of the company and both owners. Please let us know your client’s position regarding this proposal by close of business tomorrow so we can communicate it to Mr. Guariglia. Thank you, [image: Nixon Peabody LLP logo] *Seth A. Thank you. *From:* Horvath, Seth *Sent:* Thursday, June 22, 2023 2:09 PM *To:* Jessica Lesko ; Thomas Nitschke ; Michael Sanderson *Cc:* Tilghman, Richard *Subject:* RE: Rowboat - Zangrilli/Gauriglia - Request for Phone Call Dear Counsel: We are in the process of discussing with our client the terms of a buyout offer for Mr. Zangrilli’s ownership interest in Rowboat Creative LLC. We expect to be in a position to reach out to you regarding the offer by early next week. Meanwhile, our client is concerned about protecting the company’s financial well-being and would like to reach an agreement regarding draws, and credit-card usage, while the discussions regarding the buyout are pending. Mr. Guariglia’s immediate concerns are (1) ensuring that the company is able to meet its payroll obligations this coming Tuesday, as well as on future occasions; (2) ensuring that the company is able to make its upcoming rent payment on July 1; and (3) ensuring that the company is able to make required payments to its vendors. To that end, please let us know if, between now and July 31, 2023, Mr. Zangrilli will (1) agree not to incur any charges on Rowboat’s corporate credit card without first consulting with Mr. Guariglia and (2) agree not to process any distributions to himself from the company’s funds unless the distributions are mutually agreed upon and equal. Mr. Guariglia will reciprocate. These arrangements are in the best interests of the company and both owners. PRICE FIXING WITH WIFE.body: Good Afternoon, While we are appreciative of everything you are sending, what we really need is a valuation of the company and a clear idea of your overall goal. Without context, it is difficult to see how they are price fixing in these emails. Regardless, there is little to nothing we can do civilly regarding any alleged price fixing or criminal activities. That said, if he is undercutting prices for his wife, we may be able to use that information during negotiations. In order to do so, we will need to know the customary prices for those items and see whatever documentation is available regarding these transactions. We are also concerned with how you are obtaining emails that you are not copied on. Is there a Rowboat policy that allows you to audit Lucas's email account? Please ensure that you are not taking any actions that could weaken our position during subsequent negotiations and/or litigation. Please let us know if you have any questions. Sincerely, *Michael T. Rowboat Creative LLC -- Letter from Nixo.title: RE: Rowboat Creative LLC -- Letter from Nixon Peabody LLP to Joseph Zangrilli
Rowboat Creative LLC -- Letter from Nixo.body: Dear Mr. Zangrilli: As stated in my April 3, 2023, letter, Nixon Peabody LLP represents Lucas Guariglia. Regrettably, we’ve been advised that you recently withdrew a lump sum of $8,000 from one of Rowboat Creative LLC’s bank accounts. You made the withdrawal against the recommendation of Rowboat’s accounting team and CPA, who have previously expressed their concern to you that failing to follow proper protocols for withdrawing funds could result in negative tax consequences for the company. You also made the withdrawal without Mr. Guariglia’s knowledge or agreement. Given the current efforts being made to secure Rowboat’s financial standing, the withdrawal was, at best, ill-advised and, at worst, a bad-faith breach of your fiduciary obligations to the company and Mr. Guariglia. As of the date of this email, Rowboat’s bank and accountants have been notified of the unauthorized $8,000 withdrawal. Please note that, in order to protect the company’s financial well-being, steps have been taken to restrict your access to the company’s accounts to avoid further unapproved withdrawals. Consistent with your and Mr. Guariglia’s prior course of dealing and prior understanding, all future distributions, by you or by Mr. Guariglia, must be mutually agreed upon, equal, and nondetrimental to the company’s finances. As of the date of this email, no further distributions will be authorized or made to you until Mr. Guariglia’s personal loans to the company are paid back or the company is solvent. If you deviate from this arrangement, it will place the company in financial jeopardy. Mr. Guariglia reserves all rights in this regard. In addition, your aggressive behavior continues to compromise your partnership with Mr. Guariglia and impede Rowboat’s day-to-day operations. This refers specifically to your outbursts at Rowboat’s May 22, 2023, team meeting and your nonconsensual recording of the meeting. Mr. Guariglia will not tolerate any additional behavior like this. If you are unable to work collaboratively with him, we are prepared to file a lawsuit against you for damages. Mr. Guariglia has continued to propose options for neutral discussions between the two of you. You have remained combative and continued to refuse these opportunities. We urge you to consider the ramifications of dissolving the company, including but not limited to the fact that you and Mr. Guariglia each have personally guaranteed substantial debts owed by Rowboat. Dissolving the company could result in considerable debt liability for both you and Mr. Guariglia personally. It could also result in litigation if there are insufficient funds to pay Rowboat’s creditors. In addition, dissolving the company would eliminate it as a source of revenue to support your and Mr. Guariglia’s financial responsibilities. Mr. Guariglia remains open to productive discussions about potential next steps, including but not limited to: Marketing and selling the company; You buying out Mr. Guariglia; Mr. Guariglia buying out you; or Dissolution. Please contact me if you have any questions. Thank you, Seth A. Horvath Partner sahorvath@nixonpeabody.com T/ 312.977.4443 M/ 312.714.7988 F/ 844.560.8137 Nixon Peabody LLP 70 West Madison, Suite 5200, Chicago, IL 60602-4378 nixonpeabody.com @NixonPeabodyLLP This email message and any attachments are confidential and may be protected by the attorney/client or other applicable privileges. The information is intended to be conveyed only to the designated recipient(s) of the message. I can only assume he is doing to create some sort of physical altercation. (Obviously I said I didn’t approve). I am going to be hanging at the shop more to make sure he doesn’t just send someone to terrorize the team. Can you call me today? This guy.body: Good Morning, We are in receipt of your documents and emails sent over the weekend and last week. Based on the correspondence you received from Lucas's attorney, Seth Horvath of Nixon Peabody, on April 3, 2023, we believe that we need to get into contact with him as soon as possible. We are concerned that Lucas may prepare dissolution documents absent a response. We are concerned that you did not provide the letter from Mr. Horvath when we first started discussing this issue. The contents of that correspondence are obviously pertinent as Lucas is citing a breakdown of the professional relationship between you two. Additionally, Lucas is offering either a sale or buyout, which are avenues we have been discussing with you as a potential resolution of this issue. In the future, please send us any and all correspondence you receive as soon as you receive it. Furthermore, please send us complete text chains and emails rather than providing portions. We need all of the pertinent information to properly represent your interests in this matter. Please let us know your availability for a call to discuss potential terms for either a buyout or sale, so we can respond to Mr. Horvath without further delay. Sincerely, *Michael T. ZANGRILLI Rowboat Issue PLEASE HELP!.body: Tom, https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1154PQjJQgp9R0cRp-We-0K1mHW5ED5FgW-nUzZ0el_E/edit#gid=1095347979￼ I put together my case info in a google doc that I thought would simplify the issue I have with my biz partner. He has gone to my accountants and fed them fabricated stories and clearly has something going on with the secret conversations (he actually said in an email the accountants were copied on that he does not have to share any financial info or conversations he’s had with our accoutant about the business. (I have more evidence in the HIDDEN tabs on this file, didn’t want to blow your mind with too much at once.) - Running events with his wife for his wife’s employer CONSTELLATION (RBC client) without my knowledge (he admits to it, on audio and text) - Cost of goods on wife’s orders are over 70% cost of goods (sometimes with profit in negative, refunding what was never paid) - Accounting says 75% cost of goods is our main issue - Accounting says second issue is we need to make 59K per week out we are in debt - LG literally says multiple times in accounting meetings that he could “care less about sales goals" (audio and transcripts on google doc) - LG tells JZ he does not want to fix RBC financial problems due to having other paths open in the future (audio and transcripts on google doc) - LG taunts JZ saying he will destroy RBC despite JZ agreeing to work with lawyers and mediators to settle - LG took away sales accounts away from last sales person Jay Goebel and gave to his wife (LG admits in text on doc) - After becoming sole sales person LG asks for more of the biz due to value as sole salesperson - JZ is still not on the RBC bank account - LG stated in accounting meeting that he will move for dissolution no JZ on account - LG admits to all fabricated stories told to accounting says “ITS JUST BUSINESS" (to get them to go along with dismantling RBC) audio and transcript on google doc. - LG has started a pizza biz and a client (who LG went on to manage) called to let me know that LG asked him to invest - LG admits to making items in china with wife and selling to customers - LG manages another RBC client openly - LG has said in writing several times that he will destroy the business on purpose. Destructive I have also attached the two last emails with our accountants. I ask if LG has been having meetings with out me and LG jumps in and says we need layoffs and says it is accountants idea without letting her respond. ￼￼ PLEASE HELP! I have attached a little instruction screenshot below. ￼
